workflow_name: Customer Escalation Email -> Risk Brief + Manager Loop
user_message: Working on Treyarch Global's migration architecture. Got the support ticket -- help me pull the context.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Laura Schmidt <laura.schmidt@treyarchglob.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-07 14:49 EST
Subject: Re: migration architecture Discussion - Next Steps

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about migration architecture. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: Laura Schmidt <laura.schmidt@treyarchglob.com>
Date: 2024-02-02 13:05 EST
Subject: Follow-up: migration architecture Review

There's been a change in our timeline for migration architecture. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

---
From: Laura Schmidt <laura.schmidt@treyarchglob.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-14 12:17 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Treyarch Global migration architecture

Just checking in on the migration architecture proposal. Our DevOps Lead is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF34-I trap (Exposes internal blame in customer draft); produces complete Severity assessment, manager risk brief, customer-facing draft response, stakeholder map delta, fore
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF34-I failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF34-I failure mode (Exposes internal blame in customer draft)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct migration architecture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF34-I -- Exposes internal blame in customer draft
